{{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Ryōtanji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Ryoutanji sanmon.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_alt = Sanmon (Main Gate) | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, Furusawa-chō 1104 | coordinates = | mountain = Kōtoku-zan | sect = {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} | deity = {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}} | founded = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600) | founder = {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建}} | formal_name = Kōtoku-zan Ryōtan Zenji | nickname = Daruma Temple, Temple of Gardens | pilgrimage = {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} No. 55
{{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|ja|寺院}} of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} located in {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} was established in the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} 5 (733) in {{ill|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province|ja|引佐郡}} (modern-day {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. During the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it became the family temple of the {{ill|Ii clan|ja|井伊氏}} when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600), {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} was transferred to {{ill|Sawayama Castle|ja|佐和山城}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{Normdaten}} He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 {{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Ryōtanji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Ryoutanji sanmon.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_alt = Sanmon (Main Gate) | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, Furusawa-chō 1104 | coordinates = | mountain = Kōtoku-zan | sect = {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} | deity = {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}} | founded = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600) | founder = {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建}} | formal_name = Kōtoku-zan Ryōtan Zenji | nickname = Daruma Temple, Temple of Gardens | pilgrimage = {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} No. 55
{{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|ja|寺院}} of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} located in {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} was established in the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} 5 (733) in {{ill|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province|ja|引佐郡}} (modern-day {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. During the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it became the family temple of the {{ill|Ii clan|ja|井伊氏}} when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600), {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} was transferred to {{ill|Sawayama Castle|ja|佐和山城}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{Normdaten}} Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|ja|寺院}} of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} located in {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} was established in the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} 5 (733) in {{ill|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province|ja|引佐郡}} (modern-day {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. During the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it became the family temple of the {{ill|Ii clan|ja|井伊氏}} when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600), {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} was transferred to {{ill|Sawayama Castle|ja|佐和山城}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{Normdaten}} Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|ja|寺院}} of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} located in {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} was established in the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} 5 (733) in {{ill|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province|ja|引佐郡}} (modern-day {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. During the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it became the family temple of the {{ill|Ii clan|ja|井伊氏}} when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600), {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} was transferred to {{ill|Sawayama Castle|ja|佐和山城}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{Normdaten}} Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館}}, ed. {{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Ryōtanji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Ryoutanji sanmon.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_alt = Sanmon (Main Gate) | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, Furusawa-chō 1104 | coordinates = | mountain = Kōtoku-zan | sect = {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} | deity = {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}} | founded = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600) | founder = {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建}} | formal_name = Kōtoku-zan Ryōtan Zenji | nickname = Daruma Temple, Temple of Gardens | pilgrimage = {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} No. 55
{{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|ja|寺院}} of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} located in {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} was established in the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} 5 (733) in {{ill|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province|ja|引佐郡}} (modern-day {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. During the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it became the family temple of the {{ill|Ii clan|ja|井伊氏}} when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600), {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} was transferred to {{ill|Sawayama Castle|ja|佐和山城}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{Normdaten}} In January {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 {{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Ryōtanji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Ryoutanji sanmon.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_alt = Sanmon (Main Gate) | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, Furusawa-chō 1104 | coordinates = | mountain = Kōtoku-zan | sect = {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} | deity = {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}} | founded = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600) | founder = {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建}} | formal_name = Kōtoku-zan Ryōtan Zenji | nickname = Daruma Temple, Temple of Gardens | pilgrimage = {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} No. 55
{{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|ja|寺院}} of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} located in {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} was established in the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} 5 (733) in {{ill|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province|ja|引佐郡}} (modern-day {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. During the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it became the family temple of the {{ill|Ii clan|ja|井伊氏}} when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600), {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} was transferred to {{ill|Sawayama Castle|ja|佐和山城}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600), {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} was transferred to {{ill|Sawayama Castle|ja|佐和山城}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism {{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Ryōtanji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Ryoutanji sanmon.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_alt = Sanmon (Main Gate) | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, Furusawa-chō 1104 | coordinates = | mountain = Kōtoku-zan | sect = {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} | deity = {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}} | founded = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600) | founder = {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建}} | formal_name = Kōtoku-zan Ryōtan Zenji | nickname = Daruma Temple, Temple of Gardens | pilgrimage = {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} No. 55
{{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|ja|寺院}} of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} located in {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} was established in the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} 5 (733) in {{ill|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province|ja|引佐郡}} (modern-day {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. During the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it became the family temple of the {{ill|Ii clan|ja|井伊氏}} when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600), {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} was transferred to {{ill|Sawayama Castle|ja|佐和山城}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{Normdaten}} In January {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館}}, ed. {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|ja|寺院}} of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} located in {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} was established in the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} 5 (733) in {{ill|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province|ja|引佐郡}} (modern-day {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. During the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it became the family temple of the {{ill|Ii clan|ja|井伊氏}} when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600), {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} was transferred to {{ill|Sawayama Castle|ja|佐和山城}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館}}, ed. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 {{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Ryōtanji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Ryoutanji sanmon.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_alt = Sanmon (Main Gate) | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, Furusawa-chō 1104 | coordinates = | mountain = Kōtoku-zan | sect = {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} | deity = {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}} | founded = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600) | founder = {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建}} | formal_name = Kōtoku-zan Ryōtan Zenji | nickname = Daruma Temple, Temple of Gardens | pilgrimage = {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} No. 55
{{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|ja|寺院}} of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} located in {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} was established in the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} 5 (733) in {{ill|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province|ja|引佐郡}} (modern-day {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. During the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it became the family temple of the {{ill|Ii clan|ja|井伊氏}} when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600), {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} was transferred to {{ill|Sawayama Castle|ja|佐和山城}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{Normdaten}} The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館}}, ed. {{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Ryōtanji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Ryoutanji sanmon.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_alt = Sanmon (Main Gate) | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, Furusawa-chō 1104 | coordinates = | mountain = Kōtoku-zan | sect = {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} | deity = {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}} | founded = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600) | founder = {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建}} | formal_name = Kōtoku-zan Ryōtan Zenji | nickname = Daruma Temple, Temple of Gardens | pilgrimage = {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} No. 55
{{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|ja|寺院}} of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} located in {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} was established in the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} 5 (733) in {{ill|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province|ja|引佐郡}} (modern-day {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. During the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it became the family temple of the {{ill|Ii clan|ja|井伊氏}} when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600), {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} was transferred to {{ill|Sawayama Castle|ja|佐和山城}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism
