{{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Ryōtanji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Ryoutanji sanmon.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_alt = Sanmon (Main Gate) | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, Furusawa-chō 1104 | coordinates = | mountain = Kōtoku-zan | sect = {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} | deity = {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}} | founded = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600) | founder = {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建}} | formal_name = Kōtoku-zan Ryōtan Zenji | nickname = Daruma Temple, Temple of Gardens | pilgrimage = {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} No. 55
{{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|ja|寺院}} of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} located in {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} was established in the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} 5 (733) in {{ill|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province|ja|引佐郡}} (modern-day {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. During the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it became the family temple of the {{ill|Ii clan|ja|井伊氏}} when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600), {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} was transferred to {{ill|Sawayama Castle|ja|佐和山城}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989.== See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{Normdaten}} Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989.== See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 {{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Ryōtanji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Ryoutanji sanmon.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_alt = Sanmon (Main Gate) | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, Furusawa-chō 1104 | coordinates = | mountain = Kōtoku-zan | sect = {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} | deity = {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}} | founded = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600) | founder = {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建}} | formal_name = Kōtoku-zan Ryōtan Zenji | nickname = Daruma Temple, Temple of Gardens | pilgrimage = {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} No. 55
{{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|ja|寺院}} of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} located in {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} was established in the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} 5 (733) in {{ill|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province|ja|引佐郡}} (modern-day {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. During the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it became the family temple of the {{ill|Ii clan|ja|井伊氏}} when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600), {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} was transferred to {{ill|Sawayama Castle|ja|佐和山城}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also! == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{Normdaten}} Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism {{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Ryōtanji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Ryoutanji sanmon.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_alt = Sanmon (Main Gate) | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, Furusawa-chō 1104 | coordinates = | mountain = Kōtoku-zan | sect = {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} | deity = {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}} | founded = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600) | founder = {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建}} | formal_name = Kōtoku-zan Ryōtan Zenji | nickname = Daruma Temple, Temple of Gardens | pilgrimage = {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} No. 55
{{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|ja|寺院}} of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} located in {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} was established in the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} 5 (733) in {{ill|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province|ja|引佐郡}} (modern-day {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. During the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it became the family temple of the {{ill|Ii clan|ja|井伊氏}} when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600), {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} was transferred to {{ill|Sawayama Castle|ja|佐和山城}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{Normdaten}} A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}}. Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館}}, ed. {{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Ryōtanji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Ryoutanji sanmon.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_alt = Sanmon (Main Gate) | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, Furusawa-chō 1104 | coordinates = | mountain = Kōtoku-zan | sect = {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} | deity = {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}} | founded = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600) | founder = {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建}} | formal_name = Kōtoku-zan Ryōtan Zenji | nickname = Daruma Temple, Temple of Gardens | pilgrimage = {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} No. 55
{{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|ja|寺院}} of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} located in {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} was established in the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} 5 (733) in {{ill|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province|ja|引佐郡}} (modern-day {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. During the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it became the family temple of the {{ill|Ii clan|ja|井伊氏}} when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派}} during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600), {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} was transferred to {{ill|Sawayama Castle|ja|佐和山城}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Myoshinji school of Rinzai Zen temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hikone]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hikone]] [[Category:Temples associated with the Ii clan|Ryōtanji]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)]] [[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ryōtanji Hikone}}

''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政|12=draft}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|12=draft}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Myoshinji school of Rinzai Zen temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hikone]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hikone]] [[Category:Temples associated with the Ii clan|Ryōtanji]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)]] [[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ryōtanji Hikone}}

{{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Ryōtanji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Ryoutanji sanmon.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_alt = Sanmon (Main Gate) | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市|12=draft}}, Furusawa-chō 1104 | coordinates = | mountain = Kōtoku-zan | sect = {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|12=draft}} | deity = {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|12=draft}} | founded = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|12=draft}} 5 (1600) | founder = {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|12=draft}} | formal_name = Kōtoku-zan Ryōtan Zenji | nickname = Daruma Temple, Temple of Gardens | pilgrimage = {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|12=draft}} No. 55
{{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|12=draft}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|ja|寺院|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft}}. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|12=draft}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)|12=draft}} was established in the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平|12=draft}} 5 (733) in {{ill|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province|ja|引佐郡|12=draft}} (modern-day {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku|ja|浜名区|12=draft}}, Inasa-chō) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft}}. During the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, it became the family temple of the {{ill|Ii clan|ja|井伊氏|12=draft}} when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|12=draft}} during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft}} under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|12=draft}} 5 (1600), {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政|12=draft}} was transferred to {{ill|Sawayama Castle|ja|佐和山城|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国|12=draft}}. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|12=draft}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|12=draft}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣|12=draft}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩|12=draft}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝|12=draft}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩|12=draft}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼|12=draft}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮|12=draft}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|12=draft}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石|12=draft}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州|12=draft}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和|12=draft}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼|12=draft}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保|12=draft}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成|12=draft}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六|12=draft}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲|12=draft}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|12=draft}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|12=draft}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|12=draft}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|12=draft}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社|12=draft}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|12=draft}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線|12=draft}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅|12=draft}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス|12=draft}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館|12=draft}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政|12=draft}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|12=draft}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Myoshinji school of Rinzai Zen temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hikone]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hikone]] [[Category:Temples associated with the Ii clan|Ryōtanji]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)]] [[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ryōtanji Hikone}}

The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)|12=draft}} was established in the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平|12=draft}} 5 (733) in {{ill|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province|ja|引佐郡|12=draft}} (modern-day {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku|ja|浜名区|12=draft}}, Inasa-chō) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft}}. During the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, it became the family temple of the {{ill|Ii clan|ja|井伊氏|12=draft}} when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|12=draft}} during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft}} under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|12=draft}} 5 (1600), {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政|12=draft}} was transferred to {{ill|Sawayama Castle|ja|佐和山城|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国|12=draft}}. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|12=draft}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|12=draft}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣|12=draft}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩|12=draft}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝|12=draft}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩|12=draft}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼|12=draft}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮|12=draft}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|12=draft}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石|12=draft}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州|12=draft}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和|12=draft}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼|12=draft}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保|12=draft}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成|12=draft}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六|12=draft}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲|12=draft}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|12=draft}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|12=draft}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|12=draft}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|12=draft}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社|12=draft}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|12=draft}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線|12=draft}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅|12=draft}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス|12=draft}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館|12=draft}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政|12=draft}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|12=draft}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Myoshinji school of Rinzai Zen temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hikone]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hikone]] [[Category:Temples associated with the Ii clan|Ryōtanji]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)]] [[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ryōtanji Hikone}}

{{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Myoshinji school of Rinzai Zen temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hikone]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hikone]] [[Category:Temples associated with the Ii clan|Ryōtanji]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)]] [[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ryōtanji Hikone}}

{{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Ryōtanji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Ryoutanji sanmon.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_alt = Sanmon (Main Gate) | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Furusawa-chō 1104 | coordinates = | mountain = Kōtoku-zan | sect = {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | deity = {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founded = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (1600) | founder = {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | formal_name = Kōtoku-zan Ryōtan Zenji | nickname = Daruma Temple, Temple of Gardens | pilgrimage = {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} No. 55
{{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|ja|寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was established in the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (733) in {{ill|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province|ja|引佐郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (modern-day {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku|ja|浜名区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Inasa-chō) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it became the family temple of the {{ill|Ii clan|ja|井伊氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (1600), {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was transferred to {{ill|Sawayama Castle|ja|佐和山城|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Myoshinji school of Rinzai Zen temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hikone]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hikone]] [[Category:Temples associated with the Ii clan|Ryōtanji]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)]] [[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ryōtanji Hikone}}

revision.text:
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 龍潭寺 |画像 = Ryoutanji sanmon.jpg |画像説明 = 山門 |所在地 = [[滋賀県]][[彦根市]]古沢町1104 |位置 = |山号 = 弘徳山 |宗派 = [[臨済宗妙心寺派]] |本尊 = [[釈迦如来]] |創建年 = [[慶長]]5年（[[1600年]]） |開山 = [[昊天宗建]] |正式名 = 弘徳山 龍潭禅寺 |別称 = [[だるま]]寺、庭の寺 |札所等 = [[びわ湖百八霊場]]第55番
[[井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり]]第9番 |文化財 = 方丈[[襖絵]]56面（市指定有形[[文化財]]）
書院東庭庭園（市の[[名勝]]） }} '''龍潭寺'''（りょうたんじ）は、[[滋賀県]][[彦根市]]にある[[臨済宗妙心寺派]]の[[寺院]]。[[山号]]は弘徳山。[[本尊]]は[[釈迦如来]]。「だるま寺」や「庭の寺」とも呼ばれる。 == 歴史 == [[龍潭寺 (浜松市)|龍潭寺]]は、[[天平]]5年（[[733年]]）[[遠江国]][[引佐郡]]（現・[[静岡県]][[浜松市]][[浜名区]][[引佐町]]）に[[行基]]によって開かれたと伝えられる。[[平安時代]]中期[[一条天皇]]のころに[[井伊共保]]がここで葬られたことから、[[井伊氏]]の[[菩提寺]]となった。[[臨済宗妙心寺派]]の寺院となったのは[[室町時代]]末期[[井伊直平]]の代のようである。 それが、[[慶長]]5年（[[1600年]]）に[[井伊直政]]が[[近江国]][[佐和山城]]に転封となると、龍潭寺の五世[[昊天宗建]][[禅師]]を当地に招いて遠江国から分寺して建立したのがこの寺である[[石田雅彦 (著述家)|石田雅彦]]著、[[井伊達夫]]監修『おんな城主 井伊直虎 その謎と魅力』（[[アスペクト (企業)|アスペクト]]、2016年）206頁。 [[佐和山]]を借景にした[[方丈]]南庭「ふだらくの庭」は[[枯山水]]庭園、[[書院]]東庭「蓬莱池泉庭」は池泉鑑賞式庭園であり、共に開山・昊天宗建の作庭によるものである。他にも書院北庭「露地庭（ろじてい）」もある。 慶長19年（[[1614年]]）1月に[[小田原藩]]主[[大久保忠隣]]は改易されると、翌2月に[[彦根藩]]主[[井伊直孝]]に御預けの身となり、忠隣は当寺で暮らすこととなった。 龍潭寺には[[禅宗]]の大学寮が併設されており、その中に造園を学ぶ園頭科（えんずか）があった。その生徒が実習として作成した庭も残っている。茶室の飄々庵（ひょうひょうあん）は[[小堀政一|小堀遠州]]や[[井伊直弼]]などが茶を楽しんだ所である。 庫裏の一角には遠江国の井伊八幡宮（[[渭伊神社]]）から勧請された渭伊大神が鎮座している。当寺の北には[[井伊神社]]もある。 毎年4月1、2日に「だるま祭り」が行われる。 == 境内 == * 仏殿 * 庭聖殿 - [[夢窓疎石]]や諸禅師、[[小堀政一|小堀遠州]]の供養塔などが安置されている。 * 方丈 - [[元和 (日本)|元和]]3年（[[1617年]]）建立。多くの[[だるま]]が祀られている。また、[[佐和山城]]にあったとされる襖がある。 * 方丈南庭「ふだらくの庭」 - [[枯山水]]庭園。[[昊天宗建]]による作庭。 * 書院 * 書院東庭「蓬莱池泉庭」（彦根市指定[[名勝]]） - 池泉鑑賞式庭園。昊天宗建による作庭。 * 書院北庭「露地庭」 * 庫裏 * 茶室「飄々庵」 - 佐和山城の城門を利用して建てられたという。 * [[井伊直弼]]の母・[[君田富]]（彦根御前）の墓 * 井伊直弼の側室・里和の文塚 * 大洞観音堂 * 鐘楼 * 山門 - [[享保]]20年（[[1735年]]）再建。 * [[石田三成]]像 * 総門 == 文化財 == === 彦根市指定有形文化財 === * 方丈[[襖絵]] 56面 - [[江戸時代]]の[[俳人]]で[[蕉門十哲]]のひとり[[森川許六]]（[[1656年]] - [[1715年]]）によるものとされる。 === 彦根市指定名勝 === * 龍潭寺庭園書院東庭「蓬莱池泉庭」 == 前後の札所 == ; [[びわ湖百八霊場]] : 54 [[長寿院]]　-　'''55 龍潭寺'''　-　56 [[清凉寺 (彦根市)|清凉寺]] ; [[井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり]] : 8 長寿院（大洞弁財天）　-　'''9 龍潭寺'''　-　10 [[多賀大社]] == 所在地 == 滋賀県彦根市古沢町1104 == 交通アクセス == * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[琵琶湖線]]（[[東海道本線]]）・[[近江鉄道本線]]（彦根・多賀大社線）[[彦根駅]]から[[湖国バス]]（[[湖国バス#城北･大藪線|城北･大藪線]]）で「彦根グリーンハイツ」停留所下車、徒歩10分。 * 同駅から徒歩25分。 ; 備考 ※[[2023年]][[3月31日]]までは[[湖国バス#彦根ご城下巡回バス|彦根ご城下巡回バス]]のうち2便（※午前・午後に各1便ずつ）が当寺院の前に乗り入れていたが{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.ohmitetudo.co.jp/page.jsp?id=5253 |title=2022年度ご城下巡回バスの運行について |access-date=2023-03-05 |publisher=近江鉄道 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220811021856/https://www.ohmitetudo.co.jp/page.jsp?id=5253 |archive-date=2022-08-11}}、同年[[4月1日]]のダイヤ改正でその設定は[[湖国バス#観光路線（廃止）|廃止]]となった{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.ohmitetudo.co.jp/bus/icoico/local/gojyoukabus/2023/ |title=2023年度ご城下巡回バスの運行について |access-date=2023-03-05 |publisher=近江鉄道 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230303112531/https://www.ohmitetudo.co.jp/bus/icoico/local/gojyoukabus/2023/ |archive-date=2023-03-03}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * [[彦根城博物館]]編集・発行 『彦根龍潭寺方丈 森川許六の障壁画』 1989年1月 == 関連項目 == * [[龍潭寺 (浜松市)]] * [[井伊直政]] * [[清凉寺 (彦根市)|清凉寺]] * [[長寿院]]（大洞弁財天） * [[井伊神社]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ 龍潭寺] - 滋賀県観光情報 * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji 龍潭寺] - 彦根観光ガイド {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:りようたんし ひこね}} [[Category:臨済宗妙心寺派の寺院]] [[category:滋賀県の寺]] [[category:彦根市の歴史]] [[Category:彦根市の建築物]] [[category:井伊氏|寺りようたんし]] [[Category:びわ湖百八霊場]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] to [[Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

revision.text:
{{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Ryōtanji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Ryoutanji sanmon.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_alt = Sanmon (Main Gate) | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Furusawa-chō 1104 | coordinates = | mountain = Kōtoku-zan | sect = {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | deity = {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founded = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (1600) | founder = {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | formal_name = Kōtoku-zan Ryōtan Zenji | nickname = Daruma Temple, Temple of Gardens | pilgrimage = {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} No. 55
{{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|ja|寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was established in the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (733) in {{ill|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province|ja|引佐郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (modern-day {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku|ja|浜名区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Inasa-chō) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it became the family temple of the {{ill|Ii clan|ja|井伊氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (1600), {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was transferred to {{ill|Sawayama Castle|ja|佐和山城|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Myoshinji school of Rinzai Zen temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hikone]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hikone]] [[Category:Temples associated with the Ii clan|Ryōtanji]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)]] [[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ryōtanji Hikone}} {{translated page|ja|龍潭寺 (彦根市)|version=103797251|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Ryōtanji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Ryoutanji sanmon.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_alt = Sanmon (Main Gate) | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Furusawa-chō 1104 | coordinates = | mountain = Kōtoku-zan | sect = {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | deity = {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founded = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (1600) | founder = {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | formal_name = Kōtoku-zan Ryōtan Zenji | nickname = Daruma Temple, Temple of Gardens | pilgrimage = {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} No. 55
{{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|ja|寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was established in the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (733) in {{ill|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province|ja|引佐郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (modern-day {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku|ja|浜名区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Inasa-chō) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it became the family temple of the {{ill|Ii clan|ja|井伊氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (1600), {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was transferred to {{ill|Sawayama Castle|ja|佐和山城|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ryōtanji Hikone}} {{translated page|ja|龍潭寺 (彦根市)|version=103797251|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Myoshinji school of Rinzai Zen temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hikone]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hikone]] [[Category:Temples associated with the Ii clan|Ryōtanji]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)]] [[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]]
