{{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Ryōtanji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Ryoutanji sanmon.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_alt = Sanmon (Main Gate) | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Furusawa-chō 1104 | coordinates = | mountain = Kōtoku-zan | sect = {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | deity = {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founded = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (1600) | founder = {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | formal_name = Kōtoku-zan Ryōtan Zenji | nickname = Daruma Temple, Temple of Gardens | pilgrimage = {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} No. 55
{{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|ja|寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was established in the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (733) in {{ill|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province|ja|引佐郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (modern-day {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku|ja|浜名区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Inasa-chō) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it became the family temple of the {{ill|Ii clan|ja|井伊氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (1600), {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was transferred to {{ill|Sawayama Castle|ja|佐和山城|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{translated page|ja|龍潭寺 (彦根市)|version=103797251|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Myoshinji school of Rinzai Zen temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hikone]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hikone]] [[Category:Temples associated with the Ii clan|Ryōtanji]] }} [[sn:Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)]] [[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]]

The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkubo Tadachika|qq=}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odawara Domain|qq=}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Naotaka|qq=}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone Domain|qq=}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobori Enshū|qq=}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Naosuke|qq=}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|qq=}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muso Soseki|qq=}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobori Enshū|qq=}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna era|qq=}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Naosuke|qq=}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōhō era|qq=}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishida Mitsunari|qq=}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikawa Kyoroku|qq=}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Disciples of Bashō|qq=}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|ja|寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhist temple|qq=}} of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|qq=}} located in {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone|qq=}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}}. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni|qq=}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|qq=}} was established in the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō era|qq=}} 5 (733) in {{ill|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province|ja|引佐郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province|qq=}} (modern-day {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamamatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku|ja|浜名区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamana-ku|qq=}}, Inasa-chō) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōki|qq=}}. During the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, it became the family temple of the {{ill|Ii clan|ja|井伊氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii clan|qq=}} when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|qq=}} during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}} under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} 5 (1600), {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Naomasa|qq=}} was transferred to {{ill|Sawayama Castle|ja|佐和山城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawayama Castle|qq=}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi Province|qq=}}. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōten Sōken|qq=}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkubo Tadachika|qq=}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odawara Domain|qq=}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Naotaka|qq=}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone Domain|qq=}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobori Enshū|qq=}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Naosuke|qq=}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|qq=}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muso Soseki|qq=}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobori Enshū|qq=}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna era|qq=}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Naosuke|qq=}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōhō era|qq=}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishida Mitsunari|qq=}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikawa Kyoroku|qq=}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Disciples of Bashō|qq=}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|qq=}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|qq=}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|qq=}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|qq=}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga-taisha|qq=}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Biwako Line|qq=}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohmi Railway Main Line|qq=}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone Station|qq=}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokoku Bus|qq=}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone Castle Museum|qq=}}, ed. Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|ja|寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhist temple|qq=}} of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|qq=}} located in {{ill|Hikone|ja|彦根市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone|qq=}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}}. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni|qq=}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|qq=}} was established in the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō era|qq=}} 5 (733) in {{ill|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province|ja|引佐郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province|qq=}} (modern-day {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamamatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku|ja|浜名区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamana-ku|qq=}}, Inasa-chō) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōki|qq=}}. During the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, it became the family temple of the {{ill|Ii clan|ja|井伊氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii clan|qq=}} when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|qq=}} during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}} under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} 5 (1600), {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Naomasa|qq=}} was transferred to {{ill|Sawayama Castle|ja|佐和山城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawayama Castle|qq=}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi Province|qq=}}. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōten Sōken|qq=}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkubo Tadachika|qq=}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odawara Domain|qq=}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Naotaka|qq=}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone Domain|qq=}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀政一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobori Enshū|qq=}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Naosuke|qq=}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|qq=}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Muso Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muso Soseki|qq=}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobori Enshū|qq=}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna era|qq=}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Naosuke|qq=}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōhō era|qq=}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishida Mitsunari|qq=}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikawa Kyoroku|qq=}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Disciples of Bashō|qq=}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|qq=}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|qq=}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|qq=}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|qq=}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga-taisha|qq=}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Biwako Line|qq=}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohmi Railway Main Line|qq=}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone Station|qq=}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokoku Bus|qq=}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone Castle Museum|qq=}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|ja|龍潭寺 (浜松市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)|qq=}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|ja|井伊直政|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Naomasa|qq=}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|qq=}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|qq=}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{translated page|ja|龍潭寺 (彦根市)|version=103797251|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Myoshinji school of Rinzai Zen temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hikone]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hikone]] }} [[sn:Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)]] [[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]]

The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|1=Keichō|2=ja|3=慶長|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|ja|大久保忠隣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkubo Tadachika|qq=}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|ja|小田原藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odawara Domain|qq=}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|ja|井伊直孝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Naotaka|qq=}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|ja|彦根藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone Domain|qq=}}, and resided at this temple. Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|ja|井伊直弼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Naosuke|qq=}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō era|ja|享保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōhō era|qq=}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|ja|石田三成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishida Mitsunari|qq=}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikawa Kyoroku|qq=}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Disciples of Bashō|qq=}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|qq=}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|ja|多賀大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga-taisha|qq=}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|JR Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Biwako Line|qq=}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|ja|近江鉄道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohmi Railway Main Line|qq=}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|ja|彦根駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone Station|qq=}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokoku Bus|qq=}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone Castle Museum|qq=}}, ed. {{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Ryōtanji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Ryoutanji sanmon.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_alt = Sanmon (Main Gate) | location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]], Furusawa-chō 1104 | coordinates = | mountain = Kōtoku-zan | sect = {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|qq=}} | deity = {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni|qq=}} | founded = [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600) | founder = {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōten Sōken|qq=}} | formal_name = Kōtoku-zan Ryōtan Zenji | nickname = Daruma Temple, Temple of Gardens | pilgrimage = {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|qq=}} No. 55
{{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|qq=}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Buddhist temple]] of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|qq=}} located in [[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni|qq=}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original [[:en:Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)]] was established in the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 5 (733) in [[:en:Inasa District, Shizuoka|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]], [[:en:Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|Hamana-ku]], Inasa-chō) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. During the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it became the family temple of the [[:en:Ii clan|Ii clan]] when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|qq=}} during the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600), [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] was transferred to [[:en:Sawayama Castle|Sawayama Castle]] in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōten Sōken|qq=}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 19 (1614), after [[:en:Ōkubo Tadachika|Ōkubo Tadachika]], lord of [[:en:Odawara Domain|Odawara Domain]], was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of [[:en:Ii Naotaka|Ii Naotaka]], lord of [[:en:Hikone Domain|Hikone Domain]], and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like [[:en:Kobori Enshū|Kobori Enshū]] and [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]]. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|qq=}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as [[:en:Musō Soseki|Muso Soseki]], various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobori Enshū|qq=}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]] 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]]'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō era]] 20 (1735). * Statue of [[:en:Ishida Mitsunari|Ishida Mitsunari]] * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikawa Kyoroku|qq=}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Disciples of Bashō|qq=}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|qq=}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|qq=}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|qq=}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|qq=}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 [[:en:Taga-taisha|Taga-taisha]] == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From [[:en:Biwako Line|JR Biwako Line]] ([[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]]) or [[:en:Ohmi Railway Main Line|Ohmi Railway Main Line]] at [[:en:Hikone Station|Hikone Station]], take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokoku Bus|qq=}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone Castle Museum|qq=}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * [[:en:Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)]] * [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|qq=}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|qq=}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{translated page|ja|龍潭寺 (彦根市)|version=103797251|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Myoshinji school of Rinzai Zen temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hikone]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hikone]] }} [[sn:Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)]] [[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Ryōtanji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Ryoutanji sanmon.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_alt = Sanmon (Main Gate) | location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]], Furusawa-chō 1104 | coordinates = | mountain = Kōtoku-zan | sect = {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|qq=}} | deity = {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni|qq=}} | founded = [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600) | founder = {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōten Sōken|qq=}} | formal_name = Kōtoku-zan Ryōtan Zenji | nickname = Daruma Temple, Temple of Gardens | pilgrimage = {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|qq=}} No. 55
{{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|qq=}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Buddhist temple]] of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|qq=}} located in [[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni|qq=}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original [[:en:Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)]] was established in the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 5 (733) in [[:en:Inasa District, Shizuoka|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]], [[:en:Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|Hamana-ku]], Inasa-chō) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. During the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it became the family temple of the [[:en:Ii clan|Ii clan]] when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|qq=}} during the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600), [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] was transferred to [[:en:Sawayama Castle|Sawayama Castle]] in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōten Sōken|qq=}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 19 (1614), after [[:en:Ōkubo Tadachika|Ōkubo Tadachika]], lord of [[:en:Odawara Domain|Odawara Domain]], was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of [[:en:Ii Naotaka|Ii Naotaka]], lord of [[:en:Hikone Domain|Hikone Domain]], and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like [[:en:Kobori Enshū|Kobori Enshū]] and [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]]. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|qq=}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as [[:en:Musō Soseki|Muso Soseki]], various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobori Enshū|qq=}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]] 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]]'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō era]] 20 (1735). * Statue of [[:en:Ishida Mitsunari|Ishida Mitsunari]] * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikawa Kyoroku|qq=}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Disciples of Bashō|qq=}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|qq=}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|qq=}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|qq=}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|qq=}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 [[:en:Taga-taisha|Taga-taisha]] == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From [[:en:Biwako Line|JR Biwako Line]] ([[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]]) or [[:en:Ohmi Railway Main Line|Ohmi Railway Main Line]] at [[:en:Hikone Station|Hikone Station]], take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokoku Bus|qq=}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone Castle Museum|qq=}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * [[:en:Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)]] * [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|qq=}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|qq=}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{translated page|ja|龍潭寺 (彦根市)|version=103797251|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Myoshinji school of Rinzai Zen temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hikone]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hikone]] }} [[sn:Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)]] [[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] [[Category:びわ湖百八霊場]] [[Category:Ii clan]]

{{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|qq=}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Buddhist temple]] of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|qq=}} located in [[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni|qq=}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original [[:en:Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)]] was established in the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 5 (733) in [[:en:Inasa District, Shizuoka|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]], [[:en:Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|Hamana-ku]], Inasa-chō) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. During the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it became the family temple of the [[:en:Ii clan|Ii clan]] when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|qq=}} during the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600), [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] was transferred to [[:en:Sawayama Castle|Sawayama Castle]] in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōten Sōken|qq=}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 19 (1614), after [[:en:Ōkubo Tadachika|Ōkubo Tadachika]], lord of [[:en:Odawara Domain|Odawara Domain]], was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of [[:en:Ii Naotaka|Ii Naotaka]], lord of [[:en:Hikone Domain|Hikone Domain]], and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like [[:en:Kobori Enshū|Kobori Enshū]] and [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]]. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|qq=}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as [[:en:Musō Soseki|Muso Soseki]], various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobori Enshū|qq=}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]] 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]]'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō era]] 20 (1735). * Statue of [[:en:Ishida Mitsunari|Ishida Mitsunari]] * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikawa Kyoroku|qq=}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Disciples of Bashō|qq=}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|qq=}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|qq=}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|qq=}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|qq=}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 [[:en:Taga-taisha|Taga-taisha]] == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From [[:en:Biwako Line|JR Biwako Line]] ([[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]]) or [[:en:Ohmi Railway Main Line|Ohmi Railway Main Line]] at [[:en:Hikone Station|Hikone Station]], take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokoku Bus|qq=}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone Castle Museum|qq=}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * [[:en:Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)]] * [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|qq=}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|qq=}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{translated page|ja|龍潭寺 (彦根市)|version=103797251|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Myoshinji school of Rinzai Zen temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hikone]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hikone]] }} [[sn:Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)]] [[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] [[Category:びわ湖百八霊場]] [[Category:Ii clan]] [[Category:彦根市の建築物]] [[Category:彦根市の歴史]]

Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|qq=}}. {{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Ryōtanji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Ryoutanji sanmon.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_alt = Sanmon (Main Gate) | location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]], Furusawa-chō 1104 | coordinates = | mountain = Kōtoku-zan | sect = {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|qq=}} | deity = {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni|qq=}} | founded = [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600) | founder = {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōten Sōken|qq=}} | formal_name = Kōtoku-zan Ryōtan Zenji | nickname = Daruma Temple, Temple of Gardens | pilgrimage = {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|qq=}} No. 55
{{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|qq=}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Buddhist temple]] of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|qq=}} located in [[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni|qq=}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original [[:en:Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)]] was established in the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 5 (733) in [[:en:Inasa District, Shizuoka|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]], [[:en:Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|Hamana-ku]], Inasa-chō) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. During the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it became the family temple of the [[:en:Ii clan|Ii clan]] when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|qq=}} during the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600), [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] was transferred to [[:en:Sawayama Castle|Sawayama Castle]] in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōten Sōken|qq=}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 19 (1614), after [[:en:Ōkubo Tadachika|Ōkubo Tadachika]], lord of [[:en:Odawara Domain|Odawara Domain]], was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of [[:en:Ii Naotaka|Ii Naotaka]], lord of [[:en:Hikone Domain|Hikone Domain]], and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like [[:en:Kobori Enshū|Kobori Enshū]] and [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]]. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|qq=}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as [[:en:Musō Soseki|Muso Soseki]], various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobori Enshū|qq=}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]] 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]]'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō era]] 20 (1735). * Statue of [[:en:Ishida Mitsunari|Ishida Mitsunari]] * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikawa Kyoroku|qq=}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Disciples of Bashō|qq=}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|qq=}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|qq=}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|qq=}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|qq=}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 [[:en:Taga-taisha|Taga-taisha]] == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From [[:en:Biwako Line|JR Biwako Line]] ([[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]]) or [[:en:Ohmi Railway Main Line|Ohmi Railway Main Line]] at [[:en:Hikone Station|Hikone Station]], take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokoku Bus|qq=}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone Castle Museum|qq=}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * [[:en:Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)]] * [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|qq=}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|qq=}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|龍潭寺 (彦根市)|version=103797251|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Myoshinji school of Rinzai Zen temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hikone]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hikone]] }} [[sn:Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)]] [[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] [[Category:びわ湖百八霊場]] [[Category:Ii clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:彦根市の歴史]] [[Category:Myoshin-ji temples]]

{{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Ryōtanji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Ryoutanji sanmon.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_alt = Sanmon (Main Gate) | location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]], Furusawa-chō 1104 | coordinates = | mountain = Kōtoku-zan | sect = {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|qq=}} | deity = {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni|qq=}} | founded = [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600) | founder = {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōten Sōken|qq=}} | formal_name = Kōtoku-zan Ryōtan Zenji | nickname = Daruma Temple, Temple of Gardens | pilgrimage = {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|qq=}} No. 55
{{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|qq=}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Buddhist temple]] of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|qq=}} located in [[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni|qq=}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original [[:en:Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)]] was established in the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 5 (733) in [[:en:Inasa District, Shizuoka|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]], [[:en:Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|Hamana-ku]], Inasa-chō) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. During the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it became the family temple of the [[:en:Ii clan|Ii clan]] when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|qq=}} during the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600), [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] was transferred to [[:en:Sawayama Castle|Sawayama Castle]] in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōten Sōken|qq=}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 19 (1614), after [[:en:Ōkubo Tadachika|Ōkubo Tadachika]], lord of [[:en:Odawara Domain|Odawara Domain]], was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of [[:en:Ii Naotaka|Ii Naotaka]], lord of [[:en:Hikone Domain|Hikone Domain]], and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like [[:en:Kobori Enshū|Kobori Enshū]] and [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]]. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|qq=}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as [[:en:Musō Soseki|Muso Soseki]], various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobori Enshū|qq=}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]] 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]]'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō era]] 20 (1735). * Statue of [[:en:Ishida Mitsunari|Ishida Mitsunari]] * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikawa Kyoroku|qq=}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Disciples of Bashō|qq=}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|qq=}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|qq=}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|qq=}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|qq=}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 [[:en:Taga-taisha|Taga-taisha]] == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From [[:en:Biwako Line|JR Biwako Line]] ([[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]]) or [[:en:Ohmi Railway Main Line|Ohmi Railway Main Line]] at [[:en:Hikone Station|Hikone Station]], take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokoku Bus|qq=}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone Castle Museum|qq=}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * [[:en:Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)]] * [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|qq=}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|qq=}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|龍潭寺 (彦根市)|version=103797251|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Myoshinji school of Rinzai Zen temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hikone]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hikone]] }} [[sn:Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)]] [[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] [[Category:びわ湖百八霊場]] [[Category:Ii clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:History of Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Myoshin-ji temples]] {{Commons category|Ryōtan-ji (Hikone)}}
