[[sn:commons:Category:Ryōtan-ji (Hikone)]][[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11679295]] Wikidata name: Ryōtan-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Ryōtanji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ryoutanji sanmon.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_alt = Sanmon (Main Gate) | location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]], Furusawa-chō 1104 | coordinates ={{Coord|35.282777777778|N|136.26672222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | mountain = Kōtoku-zan | sect = {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|1=Myōshin-ji sect|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108821667}} | deity = {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|釈迦如来|1=Shakyamuni Tathāgata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=}} | founded = [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600) | founder = {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōten Sōken|1=Kōten Sōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28690222}} | formal_name = Kōtoku-zan Ryōtan Zenji | nickname = Daruma Temple, Temple of Gardens | pilgrimage = {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|1=Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134319357}} No. 55
{{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) | prefecture= {{ill|Hikone|WD=Q386635}} }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Buddhist temple]] of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|1=Myōshin-ji sect|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108821667}} located in [[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|釈迦如来|1=Shakyamuni Tathāgata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original [[:en:Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)]] was established in the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 5 (733) in [[:en:Inasa District, Shizuoka|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]], [[:en:Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|Hamana-ku]], Inasa-chō) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. During the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it became the family temple of the [[:en:Ii clan|Ii clan]] when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|1=Myōshin-ji sect|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108821667}} during the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600), [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] was transferred to [[:en:Sawayama Castle|Sawayama Castle]] in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōten Sōken|1=Kōten Sōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28690222}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 19 (1614), after [[:en:Ōkubo Tadachika|Ōkubo Tadachika]], lord of [[:en:Odawara Domain|Odawara Domain]], was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of [[:en:Ii Naotaka|Ii Naotaka]], lord of [[:en:Hikone Domain|Hikone Domain]], and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like [[:en:Kobori Enshū|Kobori Enshū]] and [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]]. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as [[:en:Musō Soseki|Muso Soseki]], various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobori Enshū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小堀政一|1=Kobori Enshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q541040}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]] 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]]'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō era]] 20 (1735). * Statue of [[:en:Ishida Mitsunari|Ishida Mitsunari]] * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikawa Kyoroku|1=Morikawa Kyoriku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539642}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Disciples of Bashō|1=Shōmon Jittetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621978}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|1=Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134319357}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|1=Ōhorazan Chōju-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186807}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 [[:en:Taga-taisha|Taga-taisha]] == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From [[:en:Biwako Line|JR Biwako Line]] ([[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]]) or [[:en:Ohmi Railway Main Line|Ohmi Railway Main Line]] at [[:en:Hikone Station|Hikone Station]], take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokoku Bus|1=Kokoku Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563223}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone Castle Museum|1=Hikone Castle Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487933}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * [[:en:Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)]] * [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|1=Ōhorazan Chōju-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186807}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|龍潭寺 (彦根市)|version=103797251|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Shiga Prefecture]] }} [[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] [[Category:Lake Biwa 108 Pilgrimage Sites]] [[Category:Ii clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Myoshin-ji temples]] {{Commons category|Ryōtan-ji (Hikone)}} {{wikidata link|Q11679295}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]]'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō era]] 20 (1735). * Statue of [[:en:Ishida Mitsunari|Ishida Mitsunari]] * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikawa Kyoroku|1=Morikawa Kyoriku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539642}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Disciples of Bashō|1=Shōmon Jittetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621978}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|1=Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134319357}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|1=Ōhorazan Chōju-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186807}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 [[:en:Taga-taisha|Taga-taisha]] == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From [[:en:Biwako Line|JR Biwako Line]] ([[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]]) or [[:en:Ohmi Railway Main Line|Ohmi Railway Main Line]] at [[:en:Hikone Station|Hikone Station]], take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokoku Bus|1=Kokoku Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563223}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone Castle Museum|1=Hikone Castle Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487933}}, ed. [[commons:Category:Ryōtan-ji (Hikone)]][[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] [[commons:Category:Ryōtan-ji (Hikone)]][[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Ryōtan-ji (Hikone)]][[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11679295]] Wikidata name: Ryōtan-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Ryōtanji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ryoutanji sanmon.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_alt = Sanmon (Main Gate) | location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]], Furusawa-chō 1104 | coordinates ={{Coord|35.282777777778|N|136.26672222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | mountain = Kōtoku-zan | sect = {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|1=Myōshin-ji sect|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108821667}} | deity = {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|釈迦如来|1=Shakyamuni Tathāgata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=}} | founded = [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600) | founder = {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōten Sōken|1=Kōten Sōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28690222}} | formal_name = Kōtoku-zan Ryōtan Zenji | nickname = Daruma Temple, Temple of Gardens | pilgrimage = {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|1=Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134319357}} No. 55
{{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) | prefecture= {{ill|Hikone|WD=Q386635}} }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Buddhist temple]] of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|1=Myōshin-ji sect|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108821667}} located in [[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|釈迦如来|1=Shakyamuni Tathāgata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original [[:en:Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)]] was established in the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 5 (733) in [[:en:Inasa District, Shizuoka|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]], [[:en:Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|Hamana-ku]], Inasa-chō) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. During the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it became the family temple of the [[:en:Ii clan|Ii clan]] when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|1=Myōshin-ji sect|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108821667}} during the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600), [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] was transferred to [[:en:Sawayama Castle|Sawayama Castle]] in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōten Sōken|1=Kōten Sōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28690222}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 19 (1614), after [[:en:Ōkubo Tadachika|Ōkubo Tadachika]], lord of [[:en:Odawara Domain|Odawara Domain]], was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of [[:en:Ii Naotaka|Ii Naotaka]], lord of [[:en:Hikone Domain|Hikone Domain]], and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like [[:en:Kobori Enshū|Kobori Enshū]] and [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]]. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as [[:en:Musō Soseki|Muso Soseki]], various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobori Enshū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小堀政一|1=Kobori Enshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q541040}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]] 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]]'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō era]] 20 (1735). * Statue of [[:en:Ishida Mitsunari|Ishida Mitsunari]] * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikawa Kyoroku|1=Morikawa Kyoriku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539642}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Disciples of Bashō|1=Shōmon Jittetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621978}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|1=Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134319357}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|1=Ōhorazan Chōju-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186807}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 [[:en:Taga-taisha|Taga-taisha]] == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From [[:en:Biwako Line|JR Biwako Line]] ([[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]]) or [[:en:Ohmi Railway Main Line|Ohmi Railway Main Line]] at [[:en:Hikone Station|Hikone Station]], take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokoku Bus|1=Kokoku Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563223}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone Castle Museum|1=Hikone Castle Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487933}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * [[:en:Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)]] * [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|1=Ōhorazan Chōju-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186807}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|龍潭寺 (彦根市)|version=103797251|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Shiga Prefecture]] }} [[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] [[Category:Lake Biwa 108 Pilgrimage Sites]] [[Category:Ii clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Myoshin-ji temples]] {{Commons category|Ryōtan-ji (Hikone)}} {{wikidata link|Q11679295}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]]'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō era]] 20 (1735). * Statue of [[:en:Ishida Mitsunari|Ishida Mitsunari]] * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikawa Kyoroku|1=Morikawa Kyoriku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539642}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Disciples of Bashō|1=Shōmon Jittetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621978}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|1=Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134319357}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|1=Ōhorazan Chōju-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186807}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 [[:en:Taga-taisha|Taga-taisha]] == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From [[:en:Biwako Line|JR Biwako Line]] ([[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]]) or [[:en:Ohmi Railway Main Line|Ohmi Railway Main Line]] at [[:en:Hikone Station|Hikone Station]], take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokoku Bus|1=Kokoku Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563223}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone Castle Museum|1=Hikone Castle Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487933}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * [[:en:Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)]] * [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|1=Ōhorazan Chōju-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186807}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|龍潭寺 (彦根市)|version=103797251|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] {{Commons category|Ryōtan-ji (Hikone)}} {{wikidata link|Q11679295}} [[Category:Buddhist temples in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Ii clan]] [[Category:Lake Biwa 108 Pilgrimage Sites]] [[Category:Myoshin-ji temples]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[commons:Category:Ryōtan-ji (Hikone)]][[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] [[commons:Category:Ryōtan-ji (Hikone)]][[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Ryōtan-ji (Hikone)]][[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11679295]] Wikidata name: Ryōtan-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Ryōtanji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ryoutanji sanmon.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_alt = Sanmon (Main Gate) | location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]], Furusawa-chō 1104 | coordinates ={{Coord|35.282777777778|N|136.26672222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | mountain = Kōtoku-zan | sect = {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|1=Myōshin-ji sect|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108821667}} | deity = {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|釈迦如来|1=Shakyamuni Tathāgata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=}} | founded = [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600) | founder = {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōten Sōken|1=Kōten Sōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28690222}} | formal_name = Kōtoku-zan Ryōtan Zenji | nickname = Daruma Temple, Temple of Gardens | pilgrimage = {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|1=Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134319357}} No. 55
{{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) | prefecture= {{ill|Hikone|WD=Q386635}} }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Buddhist temple]] of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|1=Myōshin-ji sect|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108821667}} located in [[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|釈迦如来|1=Shakyamuni Tathāgata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original [[:en:Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)]] was established in the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 5 (733) in [[:en:Inasa District, Shizuoka|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]], [[:en:Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|Hamana-ku]], Inasa-chō) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. During the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it became the family temple of the [[:en:Ii clan|Ii clan]] when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|1=Myōshin-ji sect|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108821667}} during the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600), [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] was transferred to [[:en:Sawayama Castle|Sawayama Castle]] in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōten Sōken|1=Kōten Sōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28690222}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 19 (1614), after [[:en:Ōkubo Tadachika|Ōkubo Tadachika]], lord of [[:en:Odawara Domain|Odawara Domain]], was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of [[:en:Ii Naotaka|Ii Naotaka]], lord of [[:en:Hikone Domain|Hikone Domain]], and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like [[:en:Kobori Enshū|Kobori Enshū]] and [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]]. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as [[:en:Musō Soseki|Muso Soseki]], various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobori Enshū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小堀政一|1=Kobori Enshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q541040}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]] 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]]'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō era]] 20 (1735). * Statue of [[:en:Ishida Mitsunari|Ishida Mitsunari]] * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikawa Kyoroku|1=Morikawa Kyoriku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539642}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Disciples of Bashō|1=Shōmon Jittetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621978}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|1=Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134319357}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|1=Ōhorazan Chōju-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186807}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 [[:en:Taga-taisha|Taga-taisha]] == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From [[:en:Biwako Line|JR Biwako Line]] ([[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]]) or [[:en:Ohmi Railway Main Line|Ohmi Railway Main Line]] at [[:en:Hikone Station|Hikone Station]], take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokoku Bus|1=Kokoku Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563223}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone Castle Museum|1=Hikone Castle Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487933}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * [[:en:Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)]] * [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|1=Ōhorazan Chōju-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186807}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|龍潭寺 (彦根市)|version=103797251|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] {{Commons category|Ryōtan-ji (Hikone)}} {{wikidata link|Q11679295}} [[Category:Buddhist temples in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Ii clan]] [[Category:Lake Biwa 108 Pilgrimage Sites]] [[Category:Myoshin-ji temples]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]]'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō era]] 20 (1735). * Statue of [[:en:Ishida Mitsunari|Ishida Mitsunari]] * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikawa Kyoroku|1=Morikawa Kyoriku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539642}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Disciples of Bashō|1=Shōmon Jittetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621978}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|1=Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134319357}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|1=Ōhorazan Chōju-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186807}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 [[:en:Taga-taisha|Taga-taisha]] == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From [[:en:Biwako Line|JR Biwako Line]] ([[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]]) or [[:en:Ohmi Railway Main Line|Ohmi Railway Main Line]] at [[:en:Hikone Station|Hikone Station]], take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokoku Bus|1=Kokoku Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563223}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone Castle Museum|1=Hikone Castle Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487933}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * [[:en:Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)]] * [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|1=Ōhorazan Chōju-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186807}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|龍潭寺 (彦根市)|version=103797251|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] {{Commons category|Ryōtan-ji (Hikone)}} {{wikidata link|Q11679295}} [[Category:Buddhist temples in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Ii clan]] [[Category:Lake Biwa 108 Pilgrimage Sites]] [[Category:Myoshin-ji temples]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Ryōtan-ji (Hikone)]][[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Ryōtan-ji (Hikone)]][[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Ryōtan-ji (Hikone)]][[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11679295]] Wikidata name: Ryōtan-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Ryōtanji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ryoutanji sanmon.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_alt = Sanmon (Main Gate) | location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]], Furusawa-chō 1104 | coordinates ={{Coord|35.282777777778|N|136.26672222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | mountain = Kōtoku-zan | sect = {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|1=Myōshin-ji sect|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108821667}} | deity = {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|釈迦如来|1=Shakyamuni Tathāgata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=}} | founded = [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600) | founder = {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōten Sōken|1=Kōten Sōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28690222}} | formal_name = Kōtoku-zan Ryōtan Zenji | nickname = Daruma Temple, Temple of Gardens | pilgrimage = {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|1=Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134319357}} No. 55
{{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) | prefecture= {{ill|Hikone|WD=Q386635}} }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Buddhist temple]] of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|1=Myōshin-ji sect|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108821667}} located in [[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|釈迦如来|1=Shakyamuni Tathāgata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original [[:en:Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)]] was established in the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] 5 (733) in [[:en:Inasa District, Shizuoka|Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]], [[:en:Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|Hamana-ku]], Inasa-chō) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. During the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it became the family temple of the [[:en:Ii clan|Ii clan]] when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|1=Myōshin-ji sect|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108821667}} during the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600), [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] was transferred to [[:en:Sawayama Castle|Sawayama Castle]] in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōten Sōken|1=Kōten Sōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28690222}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 19 (1614), after [[:en:Ōkubo Tadachika|Ōkubo Tadachika]], lord of [[:en:Odawara Domain|Odawara Domain]], was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of [[:en:Ii Naotaka|Ii Naotaka]], lord of [[:en:Hikone Domain|Hikone Domain]], and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like [[:en:Kobori Enshū|Kobori Enshū]] and [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]]. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as [[:en:Musō Soseki|Muso Soseki]], various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobori Enshū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小堀政一|1=Kobori Enshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q541040}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]] 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]]'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō era]] 20 (1735). * Statue of [[:en:Ishida Mitsunari|Ishida Mitsunari]] * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikawa Kyoroku|1=Morikawa Kyoriku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539642}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Disciples of Bashō|1=Shōmon Jittetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621978}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|1=Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134319357}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|1=Ōhorazan Chōju-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186807}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 [[:en:Taga-taisha|Taga-taisha]] == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From [[:en:Biwako Line|JR Biwako Line]] ([[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]]) or [[:en:Ohmi Railway Main Line|Ohmi Railway Main Line]] at [[:en:Hikone Station|Hikone Station]], take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokoku Bus|1=Kokoku Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563223}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone Castle Museum|1=Hikone Castle Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487933}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * [[:en:Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)]] * [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|1=Ōhorazan Chōju-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186807}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|龍潭寺 (彦根市)|version=103797251|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] {{Commons category|Ryōtan-ji (Hikone)}} {{wikidata link|Q11679295}} [[Category:Buddhist temples in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Ii clan]] [[Category:Lake Biwa 108 Pilgrimage Sites]] [[Category:Myoshin-ji temples]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|en|Ōkubo Tadachika|WD=Q8081570|lt=Ōkubo Tadachika}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|en|Odawara Domain|WD=Q903467|lt=Odawara Domain}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|en|Ii Naotaka|WD=Q877175|lt=Ii Naotaka}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|en|Hikone Domain|WD=Q1062225|lt=Hikone Domain}}, and resided at this temple. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|en|Kobori Enshū|WD=Q541040|lt=Kobori Enshū}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|en|Ii Naosuke|WD=Q716940|lt=Ii Naosuke}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Musō Soseki|en|Musō Soseki|WD=Q384703|lt=Muso Soseki}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobori Enshū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小堀政一|1=Kobori Enshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q541040}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna era}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|en|Ii Naosuke|WD=Q716940|lt=Ii Naosuke}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō era}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|en|Ishida Mitsunari|WD=Q334107|lt=Ishida Mitsunari}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikawa Kyoroku|1=Morikawa Kyoriku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539642}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Disciples of Bashō|1=Shōmon Jittetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621978}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|1=Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134319357}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|1=Ōhorazan Chōju-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186807}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|en|Taga-taisha|WD=Q402091|lt=Taga-taisha}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|Biwako Line|en|Biwako Line|WD=Q804135|lt=JR Biwako Line}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|en|Ohmi Railway Main Line|WD=Q7081104|lt=Ohmi Railway Main Line}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|en|Hikone Station|WD=Q1131151|lt=Hikone Station}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokoku Bus|1=Kokoku Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563223}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone Castle Museum|1=Hikone Castle Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487933}}, ed. [[co:commons:Category:Ryōtan-ji (Hikone)]][[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Ryōtan-ji (Hikone)]][[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Ryōtan-ji (Hikone)]][[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11679295]] Wikidata name: Ryōtan-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Ryōtanji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ryoutanji sanmon.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_alt = Sanmon (Main Gate) | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Hikone, Shiga|en|Hikone, Shiga|WD=Q386635|lt=Hikone}}, Furusawa-chō 1104 | coordinates ={{Coord|35.282777777778|N|136.26672222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | mountain = Kōtoku-zan | sect = {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|1=Myōshin-ji sect|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108821667}} | deity = {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|釈迦如来|1=Shakyamuni Tathāgata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=}} | founded = {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 5 (1600) | founder = {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōten Sōken|1=Kōten Sōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28690222}} | formal_name = Kōtoku-zan Ryōtan Zenji | nickname = Daruma Temple, Temple of Gardens | pilgrimage = {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|1=Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134319357}} No. 55
{{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) | prefecture= {{ill|Hikone|WD=Q386635}} }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|WD=Q5393308|lt=Buddhist temple}} of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|1=Myōshin-ji sect|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108821667}} located in {{ill|Hikone, Shiga|en|Hikone, Shiga|WD=Q386635|lt=Hikone}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|釈迦如来|1=Shakyamuni Tathāgata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original {{ill|Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|en|Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|WD=Q11679294|lt=Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)}} was established in the {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō era}} 5 (733) in {{ill|Inasa District, Shizuoka|en|Inasa District, Shizuoka|WD=Q11487138|lt=Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province}} (modern-day {{ill|Hamamatsu|en|Hamamatsu|WD=Q185125|lt=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|en|Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|WD=Q117077169|lt=Hamana-ku}}, Inasa-chō) by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}}. During the mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, it became the family temple of the {{ill|Ii clan|en|Ii clan|WD=Q2243336|lt=Ii clan}} when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|1=Myōshin-ji sect|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108821667}} during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 5 (1600), {{ill|Ii Naomasa|en|Ii Naomasa|WD=Q1334437|lt=Ii Naomasa}} was transferred to {{ill|Sawayama Castle|en|Sawayama Castle|WD=Q1195682|lt=Sawayama Castle}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province}}. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōten Sōken|1=Kōten Sōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28690222}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|en|Ōkubo Tadachika|WD=Q8081570|lt=Ōkubo Tadachika}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|en|Odawara Domain|WD=Q903467|lt=Odawara Domain}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|en|Ii Naotaka|WD=Q877175|lt=Ii Naotaka}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|en|Hikone Domain|WD=Q1062225|lt=Hikone Domain}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|en|Kobori Enshū|WD=Q541040|lt=Kobori Enshū}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|en|Ii Naosuke|WD=Q716940|lt=Ii Naosuke}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Musō Soseki|en|Musō Soseki|WD=Q384703|lt=Muso Soseki}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobori Enshū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小堀政一|1=Kobori Enshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q541040}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna era}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|en|Ii Naosuke|WD=Q716940|lt=Ii Naosuke}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō era}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|en|Ishida Mitsunari|WD=Q334107|lt=Ishida Mitsunari}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikawa Kyoroku|1=Morikawa Kyoriku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539642}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Disciples of Bashō|1=Shōmon Jittetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621978}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|1=Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134319357}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|1=Ōhorazan Chōju-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186807}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|en|Taga-taisha|WD=Q402091|lt=Taga-taisha}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|Biwako Line|en|Biwako Line|WD=Q804135|lt=JR Biwako Line}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|en|Ohmi Railway Main Line|WD=Q7081104|lt=Ohmi Railway Main Line}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|en|Hikone Station|WD=Q1131151|lt=Hikone Station}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokoku Bus|1=Kokoku Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563223}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone Castle Museum|1=Hikone Castle Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487933}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|en|Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|WD=Q11679294|lt=Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|en|Ii Naomasa|WD=Q1334437|lt=Ii Naomasa}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|1=Ōhorazan Chōju-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186807}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|龍潭寺 (彦根市)|version=103797251|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] {{Commons category|Ryōtan-ji (Hikone)}} {{wikidata link|Q11679295}} [[Category:Buddhist temples in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Ii clan]] [[Category:Lake Biwa 108 Pilgrimage Sites]] [[Category:Myoshin-ji temples]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

During the mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, it became the family temple of the {{ill|Ii clan|en|Ii clan|WD=Q2243336|lt=Ii clan}} when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|1=Myōshin-ji sect|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108821667}} during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 5 (1600), {{ill|Ii Naomasa|en|Ii Naomasa|WD=Q1334437|lt=Ii Naomasa}} was transferred to {{ill|Sawayama Castle|en|Sawayama Castle|WD=Q1195682|lt=Sawayama Castle}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province}}. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōten Sōken|1=Kōten Sōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28690222}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|en|Ōkubo Tadachika|WD=Q8081570|lt=Ōkubo Tadachika}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|en|Odawara Domain|WD=Q903467|lt=Odawara Domain}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|en|Ii Naotaka|WD=Q877175|lt=Ii Naotaka}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|en|Hikone Domain|WD=Q1062225|lt=Hikone Domain}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|en|Kobori Enshū|WD=Q541040|lt=Kobori Enshū}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|en|Ii Naosuke|WD=Q716940|lt=Ii Naosuke}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Musō Soseki|en|Musō Soseki|WD=Q384703|lt=Muso Soseki}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobori Enshū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小堀政一|1=Kobori Enshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q541040}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna era}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|en|Ii Naosuke|WD=Q716940|lt=Ii Naosuke}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō era}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|en|Ishida Mitsunari|WD=Q334107|lt=Ishida Mitsunari}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikawa Kyoroku|1=Morikawa Kyoriku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539642}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Disciples of Bashō|1=Shōmon Jittetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621978}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|1=Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134319357}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|1=Ōhorazan Chōju-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186807}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|en|Taga-taisha|WD=Q402091|lt=Taga-taisha}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|Biwako Line|en|Biwako Line|WD=Q804135|lt=JR Biwako Line}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|en|Ohmi Railway Main Line|WD=Q7081104|lt=Ohmi Railway Main Line}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|en|Hikone Station|WD=Q1131151|lt=Hikone Station}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokoku Bus|1=Kokoku Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563223}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone Castle Museum|1=Hikone Castle Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487933}}, ed. ''Hikone Ryōtanji Hōjō: Morikawa Kyoroku's Fusuma Paintings,'' January 1989. == See Also == * {{ill|Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|en|Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|WD=Q11679294|lt=Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)}} * {{ill|Ii Naomasa|en|Ii Naomasa|WD=Q1334437|lt=Ii Naomasa}} * {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|1=Ōhorazan Chōju-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186807}} (Ōtori Benzaiten) * {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/1205/ Ryōtanji] – Shiga Prefecture Tourism Information * [https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/article/ryotanji Ryōtanji] – Hikone Tourism Guide {{Coord|35|16|58|N|136|16|0.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|龍潭寺 (彦根市)|version=103797251|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:龍潭寺 (彦根市)]] {{Commons category|Ryōtan-ji (Hikone)}} {{wikidata link|Q11679295}} [[Category:Buddhist temples in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Ii clan]] [[Category:Lake Biwa 108 Pilgrimage Sites]] [[Category:Myoshin-ji temples]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} No. 9 | cultural_properties = 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō (City-designated Tangible Cultural Property)
Shoin East Garden (City-designated Scenic Spot) | prefecture= {{ill|Hikone|WD=Q386635}} }} '''Ryōtanji''' (龍潭寺) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|WD=Q5393308|lt=Buddhist temple}} of the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|1=Myōshin-ji sect|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108821667}} located in {{ill|Hikone, Shiga|en|Hikone, Shiga|WD=Q386635|lt=Hikone}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Kōtoku-zan, and its principal deity (''honzon'') is {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|釈迦如来|1=Shakyamuni Tathāgata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=}}. The temple is also known as "Daruma Temple" or "Temple of Gardens." == History == The original {{ill|Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|en|Ryōtan-ji (Hamamatsu)|WD=Q11679294|lt=Ryōtanji (Hamamatsu)}} was established in the {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō era}} 5 (733) in {{ill|Inasa District, Shizuoka|en|Inasa District, Shizuoka|WD=Q11487138|lt=Inasa District, Tōtōmi Province}} (modern-day {{ill|Hamamatsu|en|Hamamatsu|WD=Q185125|lt=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|en|Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|WD=Q117077169|lt=Hamana-ku}}, Inasa-chō) by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}}. During the mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, it became the family temple of the {{ill|Ii clan|en|Ii clan|WD=Q2243336|lt=Ii clan}} when Ii Tomoyasu was buried there. It transitioned to the {{ill|Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshinji school of the Rinzai sect|1=Myōshin-ji sect|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108821667}} during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} under the leadership of Ii Naohira. In {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 5 (1600), {{ill|Ii Naomasa|en|Ii Naomasa|WD=Q1334437|lt=Ii Naomasa}} was transferred to {{ill|Sawayama Castle|en|Sawayama Castle|WD=Q1195682|lt=Sawayama Castle}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province}}. He invited the fifth-generation abbot {{ill|Kōten Sōken|ja|昊天宗建|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōten Sōken|1=Kōten Sōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28690222}} from Tōtōmi Province to establish this temple as a branch of the original Ryōtanji.{{Cite book|author=Masahiko Ishida|editor=Ii Tatsuo|title=Onna Jōshu Ii Naotora: Her Mysteries and Charms|publisher=Aspect|year=2016|page=206}} The southern garden of the Hōjō, called "Fudaraku Garden," is a dry landscape garden. The eastern Shoin Garden, known as "Hōrai Pond Garden," is a stroll pond garden. Both were designed by the founding abbot, Kōten Sōken. The northern Shoin garden, known as "Roji Garden," is also noteworthy. In January {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 19 (1614), after {{ill|Ōkubo Tadachika|en|Ōkubo Tadachika|WD=Q8081570|lt=Ōkubo Tadachika}}, lord of {{ill|Odawara Domain|en|Odawara Domain|WD=Q903467|lt=Odawara Domain}}, was dismissed, he was entrusted to the care of {{ill|Ii Naotaka|en|Ii Naotaka|WD=Q877175|lt=Ii Naotaka}}, lord of {{ill|Hikone Domain|en|Hikone Domain|WD=Q1062225|lt=Hikone Domain}}, and resided at this temple. Ryōtanji also served as a Zen university, which included a department specializing in landscape design (''enzuka''). Gardens created as student practice remain to this day. The tea house ''Hyōhyō-an'' was used by renowned figures like {{ill|Kobori Enshū|en|Kobori Enshū|WD=Q541040|lt=Kobori Enshū}} and {{ill|Ii Naosuke|en|Ii Naosuke|WD=Q716940|lt=Ii Naosuke}}. A shrine within the temple precincts houses {{ill|Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja|井伊八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (''Ii Daimyōjin''), transferred from Tōtōmi Province. Nearby is {{ill|Ii Shrine|ja|井伊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The annual "Daruma Festival" is held on April 1 and 2. == Grounds == * Main Hall (''Butuden'') * Garden Saint Hall – Contains memorial towers for figures such as {{ill|Musō Soseki|en|Musō Soseki|WD=Q384703|lt=Muso Soseki}}, various Zen masters, and {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobori Enshū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小堀政一|1=Kobori Enshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q541040}}. * Hōjō (Abbot's Quarters) – Built in {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna era}} 3 (1617). Features numerous Daruma statues and fusuma panels said to come from Sawayama Castle. * Hōjō South Garden ("Fudaraku Garden") – A dry landscape garden by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin (Study) * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") – A stroll pond garden (designated as a scenic spot by Hikone City) by Kōten Sōken. * Shoin North Garden ("Roji Garden") * Kitchen (''Kuri'') * Tea House ("Hyōhyō-an") – Said to be constructed using a gate from Sawayama Castle. * Tomb of {{ill|Ii Naosuke|en|Ii Naosuke|WD=Q716940|lt=Ii Naosuke}}'s mother, Kunitomi (Hikone Gozen). * Tomb of Naosuke's concubine, Riwa. * Daitō Kannon Hall * Bell Tower * Mountain Gate – Rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō era}} 20 (1735). * Statue of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|en|Ishida Mitsunari|WD=Q334107|lt=Ishida Mitsunari}} * Main Gate == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties (Hikone City-designated) === * 56 Fusuma Paintings in the Hōjō – Attributed to {{ill|Morikawa Kyoroku|ja|森川許六|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikawa Kyoroku|1=Morikawa Kyoriku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539642}}, one of the {{ill|Ten Disciples of Bashō|ja|蕉門十哲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Disciples of Bashō|1=Shōmon Jittetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621978}}. === Scenic Spots (Hikone City-designated) === * Shoin East Garden ("Hōrai Pond Garden") == Pilgrimage Routes == ; {{ill|Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|ja|びわ湖百八霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|1=Lake Biwa 108 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134319357}} : 54 {{ill|Chōju-in|ja|長寿院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōju-in|1=Ōhorazan Chōju-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186807}} – '''55 Ryōtanji''' – 56 {{ill|Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja|清凉寺 (彦根市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryōji (Hikone)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; {{ill|Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja|井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Family Fukufuku Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : 8 Chōju-in (Ōtori Benzaiten) – '''9 Ryōtanji''' – 10 {{ill|Taga-taisha|en|Taga-taisha|WD=Q402091|lt=Taga-taisha}} == location == Furusawa-chō 1104, Hikone City, Shiga Prefecture == Access == * From {{ill|Biwako Line|en|Biwako Line|WD=Q804135|lt=JR Biwako Line}} ({{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line}}) or {{ill|Ohmi Railway Main Line|en|Ohmi Railway Main Line|WD=Q7081104|lt=Ohmi Railway Main Line}} at {{ill|Hikone Station|en|Hikone Station|WD=Q1131151|lt=Hikone Station}}, take the {{ill|Kokoku Bus|ja|湖国バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokoku Bus|1=Kokoku Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563223}} (''Jōhoku and Ōyabu Line'') to the "Hikone Green Heights" stop, then walk 10 minutes. * A 25-minute walk from Hikone Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Hikone Castle Museum|ja|彦根城博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikone Castle Museum|1=Hikone Castle Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487933}}, ed.
